description_long: This is a two-story wooden cabin with a weathered, grayish exterior and a prominent gable roof covered in dark, aged shingles. The front features a wide porch with a slanted roof, supported by posts, and a set of double barn-style doors beneath the porch. The main structure is complemented by an attached single-story wing with a matching roof and two small windows. On one side, an exterior staircase leads to a second-floor door, supported by stilts, and a small landing. The windows are rectangular and asymmetrically placed, with some grouped and others isolated. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, suggesting a structure built for durability and function in an urban or semi-urban environment.
description: A weathered, two-story wooden cabin with a gable roof, front porch, and attached side wing. Features include barn-style doors, exterior stairs, and asymmetrical window placement.
description_view_0: The front view shows the double barn-style doors, the wide porch with a slanted roof, and two upper windows beneath the main gable roof.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the full width of the porch, the attached single-story wing with two windows, and the main entrance area.
description_view_2: The rear/side view reveals the exterior staircase leading to a second-floor door, a small window, and the supporting stilts beneath the landing.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the plain wall of the main structure, the attached wing with its two windows, and the overhanging gable roof.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate pitch, overhanging eaves, and shingle cladding
attribute_window_arrangement: Asymmetrical arrangement with two small rectangular windows on the front upper story, two rectangular windows on the side wing, and a single window on the rear/side near the staircase
attribute_door_placement: Double doors centered on the front facade beneath the porch, and a single door on the upper story accessed by an exterior staircase on the side
